lines.target: Here are a few more examples of regular verbs in the present, in the past and with the past participle:
lines.base: He aquí algunos ejemplos de verbos regulares.

lines.target: I hope, I hoped, I have hoped. He lives he lived, he has lived. We finish we finished, we have finished.
lines.base: Espero, esperé, he esperado. Vive, vivió, ha vivido. Acabamos, acabábamos, hemos acabado.
lines.pronunciation: .. houp... houpt... — 8 kan... il... kid... uid.. — 12 ... biin.

lines.target: They talk, they talked, they have talked. She changes, she changed, she has changed. You play, you played, you have played.
lines.base: Hablan, hablámos, han hablado. Cambia, cambió, ha cambiado. Juegas, jugaste, has jugado.

lines.target: Let's practise the past of “ do ”:
Does he smoke? Did he smoke? We don't ask questions; we didn't ask questions.
What do you do? What did you do?
Does he wait? Did he wait? She doesn't answer. She didn't answer.
“Can” and “ will” are irregular and become “could and “ would” in the past. They have no past participle.
lines.base: 'Can' y 'will' son irregulares (could / would). No tienen participio.

lines.target: I can begin now. I couldn't begin yesterday.
lines.base: Ahora puedo comenzar. Ayer no pude comenzar.

lines.target: He wouldn't work this morning, but he will now.
lines.base: Esta mañana no quería trabajar, pero ahora quiere.

lines.target: I am (I'm), we are (we're), I was, we were, I have been.

gap_candidates.target: Here are a few more examples of regular verbs in the present, in the past and with the past participle:
Let's practise the past of “ do ”: (3)
What do you do? What did you do?
Does he wait? Did he wait? She doesn't answer. She didn't answer.
“Can” and “ will” are irregular and become “could and “ would” in the past. They have no past participle. (4)
I can begin now. I couldn't begin yesterday.
